title: App-Package Structure
text: Shiny app-package R package also contains Shiny application. satisfies two requirements simultaneously: valid DESCRIPTION file, R treats package. contains launch_app() function (app.R entry point) runs app. combination gives Shiny app everything package provides: proper dependency management, documentation system, unit tests, vignettes — without extra tooling.

title: App-Package Structure
code: movexplR6/ ├── DESCRIPTION              # package identity and dependencies ├── NAMESPACE                # exported symbols (roxygen2-generated) ├── movexplR6.Rproj          # RStudio project file ├── app.R                    # thin entry point for runApp() / Run App button ├── R/ │   ├── MovieData.R          # R6 class (database + filtering) │   ├── utils.R              # axis_vars constant │   ├── mod_filters.R        # filter inputs module │   ├── mod_plot.R           # scatter plot module │   ├── movies_ui.R          # top-level UI function │   ├── movies_server.R      # top-level server function │   └── launch_app.R         # launch_app() entry point ├── inst/ │   └── extdata/ │       └── movies.db        # bundled SQLite database ├── tests/ │   ├── testthat.R           # test runner │   └── testthat/ │       ├── helper.R         # shared setup (db_path) │       ├── test-utils.R │       ├── test-MovieData.R │       ├── test-mod_filters.R │       └── test-mod_plot.R └── vignettes/     ├── movexplR6.Rmd        # introduction and usage     ├── r6-class.Rmd         # R6 class design     ├── shiny-modules.Rmd    # module architecture     └── app-package-structure.Rmd  # this vignette

title: App-Package Structure
text: DESCRIPTION makes directory R package. Shiny app-package important fields Imports Suggests. Imports lists packages must installed app run. devtools::install() installs automatically. Inside package code, packages called namespace prefix (shiny::reactive(), dplyr::filter()) rather library(). Suggests lists packages needed testing building documentation. installed someone install.packages() package.
code: Package: movexplR6 Version: 0.0.0.9000 Type: Package Title: Movie Explorer Shiny App-Package Imports:     R6,     shiny,     bslib,     dplyr,     ggplot2,     plotly,     DBI,     RSQLite,     dbplyr,     logger Suggests:     testthat (>= 3.0.0),     knitr,     rmarkdown Config/testthat/edition: 3 VignetteBuilder: knitr

title: App-Package Structure
text: NAMESPACE lists symbols package makes available users. generated devtools::document() @export tags roxygen2 comments, never edit hand. Exporting module functions MovieData means users can call directly — useful composing package packages testing.
code: # Generated by roxygen2: do not edit by hand  export(MovieData) export(axis_vars) export(launch_app) export(mod_filters_server) export(mod_filters_ui) export(mod_plot_server) export(mod_plot_ui) export(movies_server) export(movies_ui)

title: App-Package Structure
text: file R/ contains one logical unit. Keeping files single-purpose makes easier locate test. global.R, ui.R, server.R. Everything files original 051-movie-explorer example now lives named, documented, testable functions inside R/.

title: App-Package Structure
text: Files inst/ copied verbatim installed package. extdata/ conventional subdirectory external data files (Writing R Extensions manual recommends non-R data). system.file() resolves path runtime whether package installed loaded devtools::load_all(): means MovieData$new() never needs hard-coded file path.
code: db_path <- system.file("extdata/movies.db", package = "movexplR6")

title: App-Package Structure
text: app.R two things: pkgload::load_all() simulates devtools::install() current session, making exported functions available without formal install step. launch_app() calls shiny::shinyApp(ui = movies_ui(), server = movies_server). two-liner minimum needed RStudio Run App button shiny::runApp(".") work. real logic lives launch_app(), .
code: pkgload::load_all() launch_app()

title: App-Package Structure
text: launch_app() package’s public API running app. ... argument passes additional options shiny::shinyApp() (e.g., options = list(port = 4321)). Note ui receives result movies_ui() (evaluated ), server receives function object movies_server (called ). Shiny calls server function per session; UI built startup.
code: launch_app <- function(...) {   shiny::shinyApp(     ui     = movies_ui(),     server = movies_server,     ...   ) }

title: App-Package Structure
text: tests/testthat/ directory mirrors structure R/. test file corresponds one source file: helper.R contains setup code testthat automatically sources test file runs: Tests need database guard skip_if(db_path == "", ...) cleanly skipped package installed yet (example, fresh CI environment devtools::install()).
code: tests/testthat/ ├── helper.R           # runs before every test file; sets db_path ├── test-utils.R       # tests for axis_vars ├── test-MovieData.R   # tests for MovieData R6 class ├── test-mod_filters.R # tests for mod_filters UI and server └── test-mod_plot.R    # tests for mod_plot UI and server db_path <- system.file("extdata/movies.db", package = "movexplR6")

title: App-Package Structure
text: standard devtools workflow applies without modification: key insight devtools::load_all() + launch_app() gives fast edit-reload-test loop: edit file R/, call load_all() console, run app — install required.
code: # Load all R/ files into the current session (no install needed) devtools::load_all()  # Re-generate NAMESPACE and man/ from roxygen2 tags devtools::document()  # Run all tests in tests/testthat/ devtools::test()  # Build and install the package devtools::install()  # Build all vignettes devtools::build_vignettes()  # Run the app launch_app()

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: movexplR6 Shiny app-package exploring movie database combines Rotten Tomatoes IMDb data. modernizes classic 051-movie-explorer Shiny example : Replacing ggvis ggplot2 + plotly interactive scatter plots. Replacing wellPanel layouts bslib (page_sidebar, card). Wrapping database filter logic R6 class (MovieData).

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: MovieData owns database connection exposes two public methods: $filter() querying -memory data, $finalize() cleaning connection.

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: initialization class: Opens DBI connection SQLite file. Joins omdb tomatoes tables. Collects result $all_movies (-memory data frame).
code: md <- MovieData$new(db_path) #> INFO [2026-07-13 19:04:42] Connecting to database: /home/runner/.cache/R/renv/library/movexplR6-8fe87c7d/linux-ubuntu-noble/R-4.6/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/movexplR6/extdata/movies.db #> INFO [2026-07-13 19:04:42] Database connection established #> INFO [2026-07-13 19:04:42] Loaded 12569 movies into memory dim(md$all_movies) #> [1] 12569    26 names(md$all_movies) #>  [1] "ID"          "imdbID"      "Title"       "Year"        "Rating_m"    #>  [6] "Runtime"     "Genre"       "Released"    "Director"    "Writer"      #> [11] "imdbRating"  "imdbVotes"   "Language"    "Country"     "Oscars"      #> [16] "Rating"      "Meter"       "Reviews"     "Fresh"       "Rotten"      #> [21] "userMeter"   "userRating"  "userReviews" "BoxOffice"   "Production"  #> [26] "Cast"

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: $filter() accepts parameters exposed Shiny sidebar returns filtered data frame added has_oscar column. Filter minimum reviews: Filter genre: Filter year range: Filter box-office range (millions): Filter director (partial, case-insensitive): Combining filters:
code: defaults <- md$filter() nrow(defaults) #> [1] 4181 strict <- md$filter(reviews = 200) nrow(strict) #> [1] 306 drama <- md$filter(genre = "Drama") nrow(drama) #> [1] 2195 head(drama[, c("Title", "Year", "Genre", "Oscars", "has_oscar")], 5) #>                       Title Year                 Genre Oscars has_oscar #> 1 Diary of a Country Priest 1951                 Drama      0        No #> 2 The Man in the White Suit 1951 Comedy, Sci-Fi, Drama      0        No #> 3           Little Fugitive 1953         Drama, Family      0        No #> 4                 Le amiche 1955        Drama, Romance      0        No #> 5                Breathless 1960 Crime, Drama, Romance      0        No nineties <- md$filter(year = c(1990, 1999)) range(nineties$Year) #> [1] 1990 1999 blockbusters <- md$filter(boxoffice = c(200, 800)) nrow(blockbusters) #> [1] 106 spielberg <- md$filter(director = "Spielberg") unique(spielberg$Director) #> [1] "Steven Spielberg" combined <- md$filter(   genre    = "Action",   oscars   = 1,   year     = c(2000, 2014),   director = "Nolan" ) combined[, c("Title", "Year", "Director", "Oscars")] #>             Title Year          Director Oscars #> 1 The Dark Knight 2008 Christopher Nolan      2 #> 2       Inception 2010 Christopher Nolan      4

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: $filter() always appends has_oscar character column ("Yes" / "") derived Oscars count. scatter plot uses color encoding.
code: table(defaults$has_oscar) #>  #>   No  Yes  #> 4023  158

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: Call $disconnect() explicitly close database connection. also called automatically via shiny::onStop() inside movies_server(). private finalize() method delegates $disconnect() connection also closed object garbage collected.
code: md$disconnect() #> INFO [2026-07-13 19:04:43] Database connection closed DBI::dbIsValid(md$con) #> [1] FALSE

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: axis_vars named character vector mapping display labels column names. mod_filters_ui() mod_plot_server() use populate axis selector inputs.
code: axis_vars #>          Tomato Meter        Numeric Rating     Number of reviews  #>               "Meter"              "Rating"             "Reviews"  #> Dollars at box office                  Year      Length (minutes)  #>           "BoxOffice"                "Year"             "Runtime"

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: mod_filters_ui(id) renders two bslib::card elements inside sidebar: one filter controls one axis selectors. mod_filters_server(id) returns reactive list current selections.

title: Introduction to movexplR6
text: mod_plot_ui(id) renders bslib::card containing plotly scatter plot movie count line. mod_plot_server(id, movies, filters) accepts filtered data reactive filter selections reactive, renders plot. Hover tooltips show movie title, year, box-office gross.

title: Introduction to movexplR6
code: movexplR6::launch_app()

what: Why R6 in a Shiny app?
title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: Shiny’s built-tools shared mutable state — reactiveValues(), reactive(), closures — work well small apps. app grows, state tends scatter across server function modules, making harder reason ownership lifecycle. R6 class addresses bundling related data operations data single object explicit public private boundaries. movexplR6, MovieData class owns everything related database: connection ($con) loaded data ($all_movies) data filtered ($filter()) connection closed ($disconnect()) Shiny server just calls methods object; never touches connection SQL directly.

title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: R6 class created R6::R6Class(). two important arguments public private. key rules: public members class’s API — callable readable outside. private members implementation details — callable via private$ inside methods. self$ refers current object’s public members. private$ refers current object’s private members.
code: MyClass <- R6::R6Class(   classname = "MyClass",   public = list(     # Fields and methods accessible from outside the object     value = NULL,     initialize = function(x) self$value <- x,     show       = function()  cat(self$value, "\n")   ),   private = list(     # Fields and methods only accessible from within the class     helper = function() self$value * 2   ) ) obj <- MyClass$new(10) obj$show()      # works obj$value       # works obj$helper()    # error: private

title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: fields start NULL populated $initialize(). Keeping public lets server tests inspect without extra accessor methods.
code: public = list(   con        = NULL,   # DBI connection — set by initialize()   all_movies = NULL    # collected data frame — set by initialize() )

title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: $initialize() constructor — called automatically $new(). Two tryCatch() blocks keep failures distinct: missing database file different problem malformed SQL query. re-throw error caller (Shiny server) knows initialization failed.
code: initialize = function(db_path) {   logger::log_info("Connecting to database: {db_path}")   tryCatch(     {       self$con <- DBI::dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), db_path)       logger::log_info("Database connection established")     },     error = function(e) {       logger::log_error("Failed to connect: {conditionMessage(e)}")       stop(e)     }   )   tryCatch(     {       self$all_movies <- private$load_data()       logger::log_info("Loaded {nrow(self$all_movies)} movies into memory")     },     error = function(e) {       logger::log_error("Failed to load movie data: {conditionMessage(e)}")       stop(e)     }   ) }

title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: load_data() private implementation detail initialize(). caller outside class call directly. Collecting -memory data frame (dplyr::collect()) means subsequent $filter() calls work plain data frame — fast, repeated SQL round-trips.
code: private = list(   load_data = function() {     logger::log_debug("Joining omdb and tomatoes tables")     omdb     <- dplyr::tbl(self$con, "omdb")     tomatoes <- dplyr::tbl(self$con, "tomatoes")     result <- dplyr::inner_join(omdb, tomatoes, by = "ID") |>       dplyr::filter(Reviews >= 10) |>       dplyr::select(ID, Title, Year, Genre, Director, Cast,                     Oscars, Reviews, BoxOffice, Meter, Rating,                     Runtime, ...) |>       dplyr::collect()     logger::log_debug("Collected {nrow(result)} rows")     result   } )

title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: $filter() class’s main workhorse. public server calls every input change. defensive pattern inside $filter() uses tryCatch() re-throw, bad filter (e.g., corrupted all_movies data frame) surfaces error rather silent empty result. log_warn() also emitted result empty, useful development.
code: md <- MovieData$new(db_path) #> INFO [2026-07-13 19:04:45] Connecting to database: /home/runner/.cache/R/renv/library/movexplR6-8fe87c7d/linux-ubuntu-noble/R-4.6/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/movexplR6/extdata/movies.db #> INFO [2026-07-13 19:04:45] Database connection established #> INFO [2026-07-13 19:04:45] Loaded 12569 movies into memory  # Default call — wide-open filters nrow(md$filter()) #> [1] 4181  # Narrow by multiple criteria nrow(md$filter(genre = "Drama", year = c(2000, 2014), oscars = 1)) #> [1] 108

title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: Lifecycle management split across two methods: split? finalize() must private R6 2.4.0; making public raises deprecation warning. GC safety net. $disconnect() public can called explicitly — shiny::onStop(function() movie_data$disconnect()) — without relying garbage collection timing. $disconnect() warns (never stops) error: failure disconnect closing app produce unhandled exception.
code: # public — called explicitly by the server or tests disconnect = function() {   if (!is.null(self$con) && DBI::dbIsValid(self$con)) {     tryCatch(       {         DBI::dbDisconnect(self$con)         logger::log_info("Database connection closed")       },       error = function(e) {         logger::log_warn("Error closing connection: {conditionMessage(e)}")       }     )   } }  # private — called by R's garbage collector private = list(   finalize = function() self$disconnect() ) DBI::dbIsValid(md$con)   # TRUE before disconnect #> [1] TRUE md$disconnect() #> INFO [2026-07-13 19:04:45] Database connection closed DBI::dbIsValid(md$con)   # FALSE after #> [1] FALSE

what: Object lifecycle in the Shiny server
title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: object created per session, per reactive execution. key advantage plain reactive() reconnects time: connection opened , data loaded , $filter() operates -memory $all_movies data frame lifetime session.
code: movies_server <- function(input, output, session) {   # 1. Created once when the session starts   movie_data <- MovieData$new(     system.file("extdata/movies.db", package = "movexplR6")   )    # 2. Closed when the session ends (browser tab closed, timeout, etc.)   shiny::onStop(function() movie_data$disconnect())    # 3. $filter() is called reactively on every input change   movies <- shiny::reactive({     movie_data$filter(       reviews = input$reviews,       genre   = input$genre,       ...     )   }) }

title: R6 Classes in Shiny App-Packages
text: MovieData plain R object, can use unit tests, scripts, packages without starting Shiny session — test-MovieData.R test file demonstrates directly.

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: Shiny module self-contained unit UI server logic identified namespace ID. Modules solve two problems appear apps grow: ID collisions — two inputs named "genre" different parts app clash. Modules prefix every ID namespace "filters-genre" "plot-genre" never conflict. Coupling — without modules, server logic unrelated features shares input, output, session objects. Modules give feature scope. Every module pair functions: UI function renders HTML, server function contains reactive logic. movexplR6 two modules: mod_filters mod_plot.

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: shiny::NS(id) call inside UI function returns namespacing function. Every input output ID wrapped : server function calls shiny::moduleServer(id, ...), Shiny automatically applies namespace input, output, session, input$reviews inside module body refers "filters-reviews" global session — without module author caller think .
code: mod_filters_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- shiny::NS(id)          # ns("reviews") becomes "filters-reviews"   shiny::sliderInput(ns("reviews"), ...) }

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: server function movies_server.R acts conductor: owns MovieData object, calls $filter() derive movies reactive, passes movies filters mod_plot_server().

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: mod_filters_ui(id) renders two bslib::card elements: one filter controls one axis variable selectors.
code: mod_filters_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- shiny::NS(id)   shiny::tagList(     bslib::card(       bslib::card_header("Filters"),       shiny::sliderInput(ns("reviews"), "Min reviews", 10, 300, 80, step = 10),       shiny::sliderInput(ns("year"), "Year released", 1940, 2014,                          value = c(1970, 2014), sep = ""),       shiny::selectInput(ns("genre"), "Genre", choices = c("All", ...)),       shiny::textInput(ns("director"), "Director name contains"),       shiny::textInput(ns("cast"),     "Cast name contains")     ),     bslib::card(       bslib::card_header("Axes"),       shiny::selectInput(ns("xvar"), "X-axis variable", choices = axis_vars),       shiny::selectInput(ns("yvar"), "Y-axis variable", choices = axis_vars)     )   ) }

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: mod_filters_server(id) returns reactive list. module’s output — value hands back whoever called . Returning single reactive list rather nine individual reactives keeps interface modules simple. caller receives one object, calls filters(), gets current values .
code: mod_filters_server <- function(id) {   shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     shiny::reactive({       list(         reviews   = input$reviews,         oscars    = input$oscars,         year      = input$year,         boxoffice = input$boxoffice,         genre     = input$genre,         director  = input$director,         cast      = input$cast,         xvar      = input$xvar,         yvar      = input$yvar       )     })   }) }

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: shiny::testServer() lets set inputs inspect returned reactive without running browser: session$returned() retrieves whatever module server returned — reactive list.
code: shiny::testServer(mod_filters_server, {   session$setInputs(     reviews = 120, oscars = 1,     year = c(2000, 2010), boxoffice = c(0, 500),     genre = "Action", director = "Nolan", cast = "",     xvar = "Meter", yvar = "Reviews"   )   result <- session$returned()   result$reviews   # 120   result$genre     # "Action" })

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: mod_plot_ui(id) renders single bslib::card containing plotly output text output movie count.
code: mod_plot_ui <- function(id) {   ns <- shiny::NS(id)   bslib::card(     bslib::card_header("Movie Explorer"),     plotly::plotlyOutput(ns("scatter"), height = "500px"),     shiny::textOutput(ns("n_movies"))   ) }

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: mod_plot_server(id, movies, filters) takes two reactive arguments — filtered data frame filter selections — passed parent server. ggplot2::aes(.data[[f$xvar]]) tidy-eval idiom using string column name inside aes(). plotly::ggplotly() converts ggplot2 object interactive plotly widget, text aesthetic driving hover tooltip.
code:
mod_plot_server <- function(id, movies, filters) { shiny::moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) { output$scatter <- plotly::renderPlotly({ m <- movies() f <- filters() xvar_name <- names(axis_vars)[axis_vars == f$xvar] yvar_name <- names(axis_vars)[axis_vars == f$yvar] p <- ggplot2::ggplot( m, ggplot2::aes( x = .data[[f$xvar]], y = .data[[f$yvar]], color = has_oscar, text = paste0("", Title, "
", Year, "
$", format(BoxOffice, big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE)) ) ) + ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) + ggplot2::scale_color_manual( name = "Won Oscar", values = c("Yes" = "orange", "No" = "#aaaaaa") ) + ggplot2::labs(x = xvar_name, y = yvar_name) + ggplot2::theme_minimal() plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") }) output$n_movies <- shiny::renderText({ paste("Movies selected:", nrow(movies())) }) }) }

title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: key design point: movies filters passed reactive objects, values. module server calls (movies(), filters()) inside render functions, means Shiny knows re-run render whenever either reactive invalidates. Passing reactive (current value) lets plot module subscribe invalidation events correctly.
code: # In movies_server(): filters    <- mod_filters_server("filters")   # reactive list movies     <- shiny::reactive({   f <- filters()   movie_data$filter(reviews = f$reviews, genre = f$genre, ...) }) mod_plot_server("plot", movies = movies, filters = filters) #                              ^^^^^^             ^^^^^^^ #                          reactive object    reactive object #                          (not movies())     (not filters())

title: Shiny Module Architecture
code: md <- MovieData$new(db_path) on.exit(md$disconnect())  movies_r  <- shiny::reactive(md$filter(genre = "Drama")) filters_r <- shiny::reactive(list(xvar = "Meter", yvar = "Reviews"))  shiny::testServer(   mod_plot_server,   args = list(movies = movies_r, filters = filters_r),   {     n <- nrow(md$filter(genre = "Drama"))     expect_equal(output$n_movies, paste("Movies selected:", n))   } )

what: How the modules connect in movies_server()
title: Shiny Module Architecture
text: server function’s job wiring: creates MovieData object, calls mod_filters_server() get filters reactive, derives movies , passes mod_plot_server(). output rendering happens — fully delegated modules.
code: movies_server <- function(input, output, session) {   movie_data <- MovieData$new(     system.file("extdata/movies.db", package = "movexplR6")   )   shiny::onStop(function() movie_data$disconnect())    # mod_filters_server returns a reactive list   filters <- mod_filters_server("filters")    # movies() is derived from the reactive list   movies <- shiny::reactive({     f <- filters()     movie_data$filter(       reviews   = f$reviews,       oscars    = f$oscars,       year      = f$year,       boxoffice = f$boxoffice,       genre     = f$genre,       director  = f$director,       cast      = f$cast     )   })    # both reactives are handed to the plot module   mod_plot_server("plot", movies, filters) }

title: MovieData R6 class — MovieData
text: initialization class opens connection SQLite database db_path, joins two tables via inner_join(), collects result memory, stores $all_movies. Call $filter() obtain filtered data frame rendering. Call $disconnect() close connection explicitly; private finalize() method also calls object garbage collected. Logging handled via logger package. Set threshold logger::log_threshold() initializing class.
